feed.title: Knowledge Gathering: SPV Proof Applications In the Wild and Proposed

entry.title: Knowledge Gathering: SPV Proof Applications In the Wild and Proposed
entry.summary:
Rootstock has implemented a robust system utilizing SPV proofs to facilitate the functionality of its Bitcoin SPV node. This implementation is integral to the Bridge smart contract, which operates on a consensus mechanism and plays a pivotal role in managing peg-ins and peg-outs transactions after they achieve 100 Bitcoin confirmations. The decentralized nature of this system permits any user to contribute by submitting Bitcoin headers, which helps in constructing a comprehensive view of Bitcoin's blockchain as perceived by the Bridge. This includes maintaining records of the canonical chain and all possible forks, with provisions for extending these forks through the submission of block headers that demonstrate correct Proof of Work.
The architecture of Rootstock's system is designed to uphold high security and stability standards. Since its inception in 2018, the system has operated continuously without any disruptions, underscoring its reliability and efficiency. The codebase, rooted in Bitcoinj, supports the intricate processes required for the accurate verification of SPV proofs. Moreover, specific safeguards are in place within the SPV proof verifier to prevent Merkle node type confusions, particularly addressing issues that could arise from 64-byte transactions. This meticulous attention to detail in design and operation underscores Rootstock's commitment to providing a secure and user-centric platform in the blockchain ecosystem.
